title: Deep Linking — Open Metric Directly via URL Parameter
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to share a direct URL to a specific metric so that a colleague can open the link and immediately see that metric's detail panel without manually searching.
description:
Adds URL parameter-based deep linking to individual metrics. When a user clicks on a metric in the sidebar, the browser URL bar updates with a metric ID parameter. Loading the metrics page with that parameter opens the metric detail panel directly.

The feature includes access control validation:
- If the metric ID does not exist, the user sees an "Access Denied" message
- If the metric belongs to another tenant and has not been shared, the user sees an "Access Denied" message
- If the metric belongs to the current tenant, or has been shared with them, the detail panel opens normally
- Previous/Next navigation continues to function correctly when a metric is loaded via URL parameter
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a metric updates the browser URL with a metric ID parameter
Loading the metrics page with a valid metric ID parameter opens that metric's detail panel
Invalid or nonexistent metric IDs display an 'Access Denied' message page
Metrics owned by other tenants that are not shared display 'Access Denied'
Shared metrics from other tenants open normally
Previous/Next navigation works correctly when opened via URL parameter
ux_changes: URL bar updates with metric ID parameter as user navigates metrics. Direct URL loading opens the metric detail panel. Invalid access shows a styled 'Access Denied' error page.
middleware_changes: Metric loading endpoint validates ownership and sharing permissions before returning metric data for URL-parameter-based access.
implementation_notes: Access validation checks: metric existence, tenant ownership, and sharing status. Previous/Next navigation was particularly tricky to support with URL-parameter entry since the metric index within the table must be resolved.
comment_summary: Developer tested multiple scenarios: invalid IDs, cross-tenant access without sharing, shared metrics, and owned metrics. All scenarios validated with appropriate access control. Pushed to staging after comprehensive testing.
